paper_title: Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees
summary: Problem 3 from arXiv:2304.03567 asks whether a left-maximal DFS-tree can be computed in o(n²) time in minimally strongly connected digraphs, and what the complexity is in the general strongly connected case. The current O(n²) algorithm is identified by the authors as the complexity bottleneck of their balanced bi-tree construction. No follow-up work addressing this algorithmic question was found in a web search covering the period since the paper's appearance at STACS 2024.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is about 1-independent bond percolation on Z^n (Balister, Johnston, Savery, Scott). Completely unrelated to DFS trees or strongly connected digraphs; the internal-reference match is a false positive.

search_queries: arXiv 2304.03567 Bessy Thomassé Viennot temporalizing digraphs balanced bi-trees
left-maximal DFS-tree minimally strongly connected digraph complexity o(n^2)
temporal digraph DFS-tree strongly connected 2024 2025 site:arxiv.org Bessy Viennot
notes: No follow-up found after four web calls (two searches + two fetches). The problem is a concrete algorithmic open question: improve the O(n²) bottleneck for computing a left-maximal DFS-tree in minimally (or generally) strongly connected digraphs. The paper appeared at STACS 2024; the problem is roughly 2 years old with no resolution found.
